coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, 15 weeks out — midweek quality day. 75–90 min total with 5 × 3-min uphill efforts at threshold (Zone 4, controlled hard), 2-min easy jog down recovery. Find the steepest sustained grade you can on the road or treadmill at 8–10%. The Julian Alps will ask for ~2500m of vert in 50K — start banking that climbing-specific strength now. Keep cadence quick, eyes up, shoulders relaxed.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Gut training starts now, not race week. On today's quality session, take in 60g of carbs per hour — one gel at 30 min, one at 60 min, plus 500ml of mix with 30g carbs. Post-run within 30 min: 25–30g protein + 60g carbs (e.g., Greek yogurt + banana + honey + oats). Travel days wreck appetite cues — eat by the clock, not by hunger.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Travel + hard training = compounded fatigue. Tonight: 10 minutes legs-up-the-wall before bed, then compression socks if you're flying tomorrow. Hydrate with 500ml + electrolytes within an hour of finishing your run. Aim for 7.5–8 hours of sleep — guard the last 90 minutes from screens and work email. Recovery is the training that makes today's session pay off in September.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 107 days out. You're in the middle phase — the part nobody posts about, where the work just compounds quietly. The Julian Alps don't reward the loudest training block; they reward the most consistent one. Today's job isn't to be a hero. It's to show up, hit the workout cleanly, and finish feeling like you could've done one more rep. Trust the build.
